extraction.one_line_summary: ASAERTS Nikki won a chaotic wet finish after STEELS Stijn's late attack on slippery roundabouts was chased down in final 500m
tactical_patterns: Astana placed SANCHEZ in break while still protecting LOPEZ in peloton, hedging bets on break survival vs GC defense
STEELS attacked through technical wet roundabout with 500m left, exploiting big riders' caution but caught by sprinters' draft efficiency
Teams conceded 9+ minutes to break on transition stage before MTF, prioritizing crash avoidance over gap control
DE LA CRUZ as local rider attacked late but positioning through final corner negated earlier acceleration advantage

rider_signals.ASAERTS Nikki: positive: sprinted seated in wet conditions for efficiency while others stood, tactical maturity from Sunweb rouleur
rider_signals.STEELS Stijn: positive: timed attack for final wet roundabout exploiting height/handling advantage, nearly succeeded from non-sprinter profile
rider_signals.VAN DER SANDE Tosh: positive: third place from breakaway showed Lotto positioning skills translate to reduced bunch sprints
rider_signals.SANCHEZ Luis Leon: neutral: Astana's dual-track tactic placing him in break while protecting LOPEZ suggests team uncertainty about break survival
rider_signals.DE LA CRUZ David: negative: local rider motivation couldn't overcome poor corner positioning despite late acceleration
